sections.title: STANDARD FOR WARE POTATOES
sections.text: Adopted in 2020.
sections.retrieval_text:
STANDARD FOR WARE POTATOES
level=1
Adopted in 2020.

page_spans.text: Adopted in 2020.

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: The purpose of the Standard is to define the quality requirements for ware potatoes after preparation (e.g. brushing and/or washing) and packaging. When applied at stages following packaging, ware potatoes may show in relation to the requirements of the standard: • a slight lack of freshness and turgidity; • a slight deterioration due to their development and their tendency to perish. The holder/seller of products may not display such products or offer them for sale, or deliver or market them in any manner other than in conformity with this standard. The holder/seller shall be responsible for observing such conformity.

When applied at stages following packaging, ware potatoes may show in relation to the requirements of the standard: • a slight lack of freshness and turgidity; • a slight deterioration due to their development and their tendency to perish. The holder/seller of products may not display such products or offer them for sale, or deliver or market them in any manner other than in conformity with this standard. The holder/seller shall be responsible for observing such conformity.

sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text:
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of ware potato grown from Solanum tuberosum L., of the Solanaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Ware potatoes for industrial processing and early potatoes are excluded.

(a) Produce belonging to other varieties than marked 2 2 2
(b) Size Tolerances-off size from what is indicated/marketed 10 10 10
Note: The total of (a), (b), (c) and (d) shall not exceed the limit given in (i). Where the product is not classified the tolerances for Class II apply.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
sections.text: At all marketing stages, tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each lot for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated. Produce that fail conformity assessment, may be allowed to be resorted and brought into conformity in accordance with the relevant provisions in the Guidelines for Food Import Control Systems (CXG 47-2003).

sections.title: Minimum Requirements
sections.text: Subject to the tolerances allowed, the ware potatoes must be: • intact; • sound; produce affected by rotting or deterioration, which makes it unfit for consumption is excluded • fresh in appearance and firm; • clean and practically free of any visible foreign matter; • practically free from pests; • practically free from damage caused by pests; • free of abnormal external moisture, excluding condensation following removal from cold storage; • free of any foreign smell; • free of damage caused by low or high temperature; • free from sprouting; i.e. sprouts may not be longer than 3mm in length • free of defects affecting the appearance, keeping quality and presentation in the package, such as: o Green colouration; pale green flush exceeding one eighth of the surface area; o brown stains; o cracks cuts, , bruises or roughness exceeding 4 mm in depth; o serious deformities; o grey, blue or black sub-epidermal stains; exceeding 5 mm in depth; o hollow or black hearts and other internal defects; o deep common potato scab and powdery potato scab, of a depth of 2 mm or more o superficial common potato scab, i.e. scab spots in all must not extend over more than a quarter of the surface of the tuber. The development and condition of the ware potatoes must be such as to enable them to: • withstand transportation and handling; and • arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: Ware potatoes may be sized by diameter, count or weight; or in accordance with trading practices. When sized in accordance with trading practices, the package must be labelled with the size and method used. When size is determined by the equatorial diameter (means the maximum distance taken from the right angle on the largest axis of the tuber) of the ware potato (in mm) in accordance with the following table that can be used as a guide in an optional way: Size Code Equatorial Diameter in mm A(Large) more than 80 B(Round) 35-80 C(Long) 25-75 D(Small) 18-24 However, uniformity in size in sales packages up to 5 kg net weight may be restricted to a maximum of 30 mm between the smallest and the largest tuber.

sections.title: Minimum Maturity Requirements
sections.text: Ware potatoes must be sufficiently developed with cured skin, with account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and/or commercial type and the area in which they are grown.

sections.title: Classification
sections.text: Classification of ware potatoes is optional. When classified, the classification is done in accordance with Section 5 – Provisions concerning Tolerances, ware potatoes are classified into the following classes. • “Extra” Class, Class I and Class II.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION
number=6
level=1

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text:
The contents of each package (or lot for produce presented in the bulk transport vehicle) must be uniform and contain only ware potatoes of the same origin, variety or commercial type, quality, size (if sized) and optionally, cooking type (if indicated).
The visible part of the contents of the package (or lot for produce presented in the bulk transport vehicle) must be representative of the entire contents.
However, a mixture of distinctly different ware potatoes of different skin colours (except green) may be packed together in a sales package, provided they are uniform in quality and, for each variety concerned, in origin.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text:
Ware potatoes must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be of food grade quality, clean and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue.
Ware potatoes shall be packed in each package in compliance with the appropriate sections of the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The packages shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the ware potatoes. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.text: In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: Non-Retail Containers
sections.text: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, either printed on the package itself or on a label secured to the fastening (if the labels are placed inside the packages (string bag), this should be done in such a way that the indications concerning marking are readable from the outside). For produce transported in bulk the se particulars must appear on a document accompanying the good, and attached in a visible position in the transport vehicle, unless the document is replaced by an electronic solution in that case the identification should be machine readable and easily accessible.

sentences.original: The purpose of the Standard is to define the quality requirements for ware potatoes after preparation (e.g. brushing and/or washing) and packaging.

classification.sentence: The holder/seller shall be responsible for observing such conformity.

sentences.original: When applied at stages following packaging, ware potatoes may show in relation to the requirements of the standard: • a slight lack of freshness and turgidity; • a slight deterioration due to their development and their tendency to perish.

sentences.original: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of ware potato grown from Solanum tuberosum L., of the Solanaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.

sentences.original: Subject to the tolerances allowed, the ware potatoes must be: • intact; • sound; produce affected by rotting or deterioration, which makes it unfit for consumption is excluded • fresh in appearance and firm; • clean and practically free of any visible foreign matter; • practically free from pests; • practically free from damage caused by pests; • free of abnormal external moisture, excluding condensation following removal from cold storage; • free of any foreign smell; • free of damage caused by low or high temperature; • free from sprouting; i.e. sprouts may not be longer than 3mm in length • free of defects affecting the appearance, keeping quality and presentation in the package, such as: o Green colouration; pale green flush exceeding one eighth of the surface area; o brown stains; o cracks cuts, , bruises or roughness exceeding 4 mm in depth; o serious deformities; o grey, blue or black sub-epidermal stains; exceeding 5 mm in depth; o hollow or black hearts and other internal defects; o deep common potato scab and powdery potato scab, of a depth of 2 mm or more o superficial common potato scab, i.e. scab spots in all must not extend over more than a quarter of the surface of the tuber.

classification.sentence: The development and condition of the ware potatoes must be such as to enable them to: • withstand transportation and handling; and • arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.
classification.pattern: Subject + modal 'must be' + purpose clause + bulleted functions

components.subject: The development and condition of the ware potatoes
components.requirement: must be such as to enable them to withstand transportation and handling; and arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination

classification.justification: States mandatory quality conditions to satisfy transport and destination suitability expressed by 'must be'.

sentences.original:
S.No.
Quality Tolerances
Percentage of defective produce, by number or weight

classification.sentence:
Extra
Class Class I Class II
1 (i) Total Tolerances for ware potatoes not satisfying the minimum requirements including defects (d), of which no more than:
5 10 10
(a) Frozen, decay, soft rot and/or internal breakdown.
1 1 2
(b) Extraneous matter 0.25 0.50 0.50
(c) Soil 0.25 0.25 0.25
(d) Defects:
• cuts
• bruises or roughness
• Grey, blue or black sub-epidermal stains; > 5 mm deep
• Deep common potato scab and powdery potato scab, > 2 mm deep.
• superficial common potato scab > 25% of surface
• Sprouts >3mm
• Green coloration >1/8 of the surface area or not exceeding 2mm in depth;
classification.function: Setting quantitative tolerances and defect thresholds
classification.pattern: Tabulated tolerances specifying maximum acceptable levels for different defect types across classes

tolerance_categories: Frozen, decay, soft rot and/or internal breakdown
defect_descriptions: Grey, blue or black sub-epidermal stains; > 5 mm deep
Deep common potato scab and powdery potato scab, > 2 mm deep.
superficial common potato scab > 25% of surface
Green coloration >1/8 of the surface area or not exceeding 2mm in depth

classification.justification: The sentence enumerates specific quantitative limits for various defects and contaminants for different classes of ware potatoes, forming a detailed table of tolerances. No exact template for such detailed multi-level tabulated tolerances exists.
notes: Highly detailed tabular data; no known strict template in catalog.

sentences.original: The contents of each package (or lot for produce presented in the bulk transport vehicle) must be uniform and contain only ware potatoes of the same origin, variety or commercial type, quality, size (if sized) and optionally, cooking type (if indicated).

classification.sentence:
The visible part of the contents of the package (or lot for produce presented in the bulk transport vehicle) must be representative of the entire contents.
However, a mixture of distinctly different ware potatoes of different skin colours (except green) may be packed together in a sales package, provided they are uniform in quality and, for each variety concerned, in origin.
classification.function: Obligation for representation plus conditional permission for mixed packaging
classification.pattern: Two-part sentence combining an obligation about content visibility representativeness and a conditional permission for mixing varieties

components.visibility_obligation: visible part must be representative of entire contents
components.permission_condition: mixture of different skin colours except green may be packed together in sales package

classification.justification: Sentence includes combination of obligation and conditional permission with exceptions and conditions; structurally more complex than standard templates.
notes: Hybrid modality with obligation and permission elements.

sentences.original: The packages shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the ware potatoes.

classification.sentence: Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

components.Subject: Packages
components.Predicate: be free of all foreign matter and smell

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a mandatory requirement (obligation) that packages should not contain foreign matter or smell, structured as a direct obligation with 'must' plus a predicate describing a state to be met. There is no strong matching template available, so a new pattern is created preserving the modality and communicative function.

sentences.original: In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

classification.sentence: In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:
classification.pattern: [Referential clause indicating baseline standard], [deictic phrase indicating added provisions], [main clause stating applicability of these provisions]

components.baseline_standard: requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by stating that additional specific provisions apply beyond an established baseline standard. The structure is a referential introduction combined with a deictic phrase and a main clause of obligation. No known template matches this exact structural pattern, resulting in a new pattern classification.
notes: The sentence functions as a normative extension clause referencing another standard and adding obligations, which is distinct from typical obligation templates focused on direct action requirements.

sentences.original: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, either printed on the package itself or on a label secured to the fastening (if the labels are placed inside the packages (string bag), this should be done in such a way that the indications concerning marking are readable from the outside).

classification.sentence: For produce transported in bulk the se particulars must appear on a document accompanying the good, and attached in a visible position in the transport vehicle, unless the document is replaced by an electronic solution in that case the identification should be machine readable and easily accessible.
classification.function: Obligation for documentation plus conditional permission for electronic replacement
classification.pattern: Compound sentence combining documentation obligation and conditional permission for electronic format with requirements

components.obligation: particulars must appear on document accompanying goods and attached visibly in transport vehicle
components.permission_condition: if replaced by electronic solution, identification should be machine readable and accessible

classification.justification: Sentence contains an obligation and a conditional provision for electronic format usage; partial match to documentation templates.
notes: Hybrid obligation and permission.

However, no existing template in the catalog matches this exact structure or content, resulting in a new_pattern classification with an inferred structural pattern.

No known templates available for direct or partial match, necessitating a new pattern classification.

sentences.original: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene(C XC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables  (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.

sentences.original: The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

classification.sentence: The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
classification.pattern: Subject + modal verb (should) + verb (comply) + object (criteria) + relative clause specifying criteria source

components.subject: The produce
components.criteria_source: Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by requiring compliance with established microbiological criteria. Its structure features a subject followed by a modal verb and verb phrase describing compliance with specific criteria referenced by a guideline. No exact or close template from the catalog matches this specific compliance citation structure, resulting in a new pattern classification.
notes: The modal 'should' here conveys an obligation in normative regulatory context.
The sentence references an external standard for criteria but does not specify numerical limits, hence falls under obligation rather than limits and targets.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR WARE POTATOES
pages.summary: Página inicial do documento com título principal STANDARD FOR WARE POTATOES e código CXS 339-2020 como cabeçalho. Contém texto breve de adoção. Estrutura simples com título nível 1 e texto associado.

Página com três seções principais: 1. SCOPE, 2. DEFINITION OF PRODUCE, 3. PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY. A seção 3 possui subseção 3.1 Minimum Requirements com texto detalhado. A seção 3 continua na próxima página.

Página com seção 3 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY' continua da página anterior, com subseções 3.1.1 e 3.2 detalhadas. Novas seções 4 e 5 iniciadas nesta página com seus textos associados. Conteúdo de seção 3 continua na próxima página, indicado pela marcação de continuidade.

A página apresenta a seção 5.1 'Quality Tolerances3' com seu texto detalhado, seguida da subseção 5.2 'Additional tolerances'. Inicia a seção 6 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION' com as subseções 6.1 'Uniformity' e 6.2 'Packaging' que contém a subseção 6.2.1 'Description of Containers'. A seção 6 continua na próxima página.

A página apresenta estrutura detalhada da seção 7 "PROVISIONS CONCERNING MARKING OR LABELLING" com seus níveis de subseções 7.1, 7.1.1, 7.1.2, 7.2, e suas subseções correspondentes até 7.2.5. Em seguida, são apresentadas as seções 8 "CONTAMINANTS" e 9 "HYGIENE" com seus subtópicos 8.1, 8.2 e 9.1, 9.2 respectivamente. A seção 7 continua da página anterior (continuação) e parece continuar na próxima. As seções 8 e 9 iniciam e terminam na página atual.
